name: Tuna (SS-203)
events: 26 Jan 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.L. De Tar) departed Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the East China Sea.
21 Mar 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.L. De Tar) ended her 1st war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
14 Apr 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.L. De Tar) departed Pearl Harbor for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Honshu in Japanese home waters.
15 May 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.L. De Tar) torpedoed and sank the Japanese transport ship Toyoharu Maru (805 GRT) about 65 nautical miles off Sohuksando, Korea in position 33°34'N, 125°09'E.
16 Jun 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.L. De Tar) ended her 2nd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
13 Jul 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) departed Pearl Harbor for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Aleutian Islands.
5 Sep 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) ended her 3rd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
9 Nov 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) departed Pearl Harbor for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Solomon Islands.
27 Dec 1942 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) ended her 4th war patrol at Brisbane, Australia.
18 Jan 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) departed from Brisbane for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol of the Solomon Islands and off New Ireland.
17 Feb 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) ended her 5th war patrol at Brisbane.
4 Mar 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) departed from Brisbane for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bismarck Archipelago.
30 Mar 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) torpedoed and sank the Japanese transport ship Kurohime Maru (4697 GRT) north of Manus, Admiralty Islands in position 00°22'S, 147°46'E.
20 Apr 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) ended her 6th war patrol at Brisbane.
21 Aug 1943 After a futile 7th war patrol USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) departed from Fremantle, Australia for her 8th war patrol.
14 Oct 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Holtz) ended her 8th war patrol at Fremantle.
3 Nov 1943 USS Tuna (T/Cdr. J.T. Hardin, USN) conducted exercises off Fremantle during which HrMs Van Galen (Lt.Cdr. F.T. Burghard, RNethN) acted as target. Also A/S exercises were carried out. (1)
7 Nov 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) departed from Fremantle for her 9th war patrol.
12 Dec 1943 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) torpedoed and sank the Japanese troop transport Tosei Maru (5484 GRT) north of Halmahera in position 02°44'N, 126°14'E.
1 Jan 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) ended her 9th war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She was ordered to the Hunters Point Navy Yard for an overhaul.
24 Apr 1944 With her overhaul completed USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) left Pearl Harbor for her 10th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Palau.
4 May 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) sank the Japanese guard boat Tajima Maru (89 GRT) with gunfire north of Wake Island in position 22°06'N, 166°47'E.
21 Jun 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) ended her 10th war patrol at Majuro.
15 Jul 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) departed Majuro for her 11th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Shikoku and Kyushu in Japanese home waters.
5 Sep 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hardin) ended her 11th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.

3 Oct 1944 USS Tuna (T/Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr., USN), USS Pogy (T/Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr., USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Allen (Lt. J.A. Rowe, USNR), USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Fleming (Lt. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR), USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Widgeon (Lt. C.W. Engler, USN).
8 Oct 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 12th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Philippines.
2 Dec 1944 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr) ended her 12th war patrol at Saipan.
6 Jan 1945 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr) departed for her 13th and final war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off north-east Borneo.
18 Feb 1945 At 0900 H/18, east of Karimata Strait, USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) exchanged calls with USS Tuna (T/Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr., USN). (2)
3 Mar 1945 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr) lands supplies near Labuk Bay on the northeast coast of Borneo.
13 Mar 1945 USS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr) ended her 13th and last war patrol at Fremantle, Australia. She is now assigned to training duties.
